    rootSymbol(String rootSymbol)
    Unique identifier for the underlying asset and its associated option contracts, which reflects any changes to the underlying asset caused by corporate actions such as mergers, splits, or spin-offs by updating the root with a numerical value to reflect the adjusted terms or entity.

  • Method Details

    • classification

      public OptionRoot.Builder classification(Classification classification)
      Classification is the category of option that the option falls into These are based on how the contract was written. Only STANDARD is supported at this time.

    • classification

      public OptionRoot.Builder classification(Optional<? extends Classification> classification)
      Classification is the category of option that the option falls into These are based on how the contract was written. Only STANDARD is supported at this time.

    • deliverables

      public OptionRoot.Builder deliverables(List<OptionDeliverable> deliverables)
      deliverables is the deliverables of the option More than 20 deliverable assets are not supported.

    • deliverables

      public OptionRoot.Builder deliverables(Optional<? extends List<OptionDeliverable>> deliverables)
      deliverables is the deliverables of the option More than 20 deliverable assets are not supported.

      The penny pilot indicator of the option

      The penny pilot indicator of the option

      public OptionRoot.Builder positionLimit(PositionLimit positionLimit)
      The position Limit for a given option set at the OCC.

    • positionLimit

      public OptionRoot.Builder positionLimit(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PositionLimit> positionLimit)
      The position Limit for a given option set at the OCC.

    • rootSymbol

      public OptionRoot.Builder rootSymbol(String rootSymbol)
      Unique identifier for the underlying asset and its associated option contracts, which reflects any changes to the underlying asset caused by corporate actions such as mergers, splits, or spin-offs by updating the root with a numerical value to reflect the adjusted terms or entity.

    • rootSymbol

      public OptionRoot.Builder rootSymbol(Optional<String> rootSymbol)
      Unique identifier for the underlying asset and its associated option contracts, which reflects any changes to the underlying asset caused by corporate actions such as mergers, splits, or spin-offs by updating the root with a numerical value to reflect the adjusted terms or entity.

    • settlementStyle

      public OptionRoot.Builder settlementStyle(SettlementStyle settlementStyle)
      Indicates whether the option is designated to settle at the market's opening price on the expiration date.

    • settlementStyle

      public OptionRoot.Builder settlementStyle(Optional<? extends SettlementStyle> settlementStyle)
      Indicates whether the option is designated to settle at the market's opening price on the expiration date.

    • strikeMultiplier

      public OptionRoot.Builder strikeMultiplier(StrikeMultiplier strikeMultiplier)
      A number that identifies where the decimal for a strike price should be within the OSI. For example, in the OSI: AAPL 210416C00120000, a strike_multiplier of 1 indicates that the strike price is $120.000 and a strike_multiplier of .1 indicates that the strike price is $12.00.

      strike_multiplier is: always 1.0 for Equity Options, usually 1.0 for Index Options, but is 0.1 for “half-point” Index Options.

    • strikeMultiplier

      public OptionRoot.Builder strikeMultiplier(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StrikeMultiplier> strikeMultiplier)
      A number that identifies where the decimal for a strike price should be within the OSI. For example, in the OSI: AAPL 210416C00120000, a strike_multiplier of 1 indicates that the strike price is $120.000 and a strike_multiplier of .1 indicates that the strike price is $12.00.

      strike_multiplier is: always 1.0 for Equity Options, usually 1.0 for Index Options, but is 0.1 for “half-point” Index Options.

    • strikePriceMultiplier

      public OptionRoot.Builder strikePriceMultiplier(StrikePriceMultiplier strikePriceMultiplier)
      A number defined as 1 unit of strike value. For example, if the strike price multiplier = 100, then a strike of $17 equals $1,700. This field is used for calculating extended strike values. The number of units represented by the Strike Price Multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100 For mini options the typical value is 10

    • strikePriceMultiplier

      public OptionRoot.Builder strikePriceMultiplier(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StrikePriceMultiplier> strikePriceMultiplier)
      A number defined as 1 unit of strike value. For example, if the strike price multiplier = 100, then a strike of $17 equals $1,700. This field is used for calculating extended strike values. The number of units represented by the Strike Price Multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100 For mini options the typical value is 10

    • tradeValueMultiplier

      public OptionRoot.Builder tradeValueMultiplier(TradeValueMultiplier tradeValueMultiplier)
      A number defined as 1 unit of trade premium value. For example, if the multiplier = 100, then a premium of $1.50 equals $150. This field is used for calculating premium money extensions. The number of units represented by the multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100. In the majority of cases, the Trade Premium has to be extended to the same base as the Strike Price. Therefore, the Trade Value is usually the same as the Strike Value. For mini options the typical value is 10

    • tradeValueMultiplier

      public OptionRoot.Builder tradeValueMultiplier(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends TradeValueMultiplier> tradeValueMultiplier)
      A number defined as 1 unit of trade premium value. For example, if the multiplier = 100, then a premium of $1.50 equals $150. This field is used for calculating premium money extensions. The number of units represented by the multiplier is comprised of: 1) Number of shares for Equity Options 2) A dollar amount for Index Options The typical value of this multiplier for Equity and Index options is 100. In the majority of cases, the Trade Premium has to be extended to the same base as the Strike Price. Therefore, the Trade Value is usually the same as the Strike Value. For mini options the typical value is 10

    • build

      public OptionRoot build()
